titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/253

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=236 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:22:45.569264+00 | translation moa=237 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:22:43.995059+00

div.p:
.I. Dei Gratia Abbas De Lundors ⁊ .H. Eadem Gratia prior De Sancto Andrea . ⁊ . [blank space] . prior De lundors vniuersis Christi fidelibus Ad quos
presens scriptum peruenerit  Salutem Eternam in Domino. Nouerit vniuersitas uestra quod Cum De mandato Domini . pape . quedam causa uerteretur
Inter Abbatem ⁊ Conuentum De Dunfermelin ⁊ Magistrum Ric’. rectorem ecclesie De Hales ex vna parte . ⁊ Abbatem ⁊ Conuentum De Sancta cruce
ex Altera Super decimis de Craggis De Gorgin. Tandem De consensu partium lis inter partes mota  sic sopita amicabiliter conquieuit . scilicet . quod
predicti Abbas ⁊ Conuentus de Dunf’ . ⁊ Magister . R . rector Dicte ecclesie hales . pro se ⁊ successoribus suis Inperpetuum predicte liti renuncia
uerunt . sub hac forma . scilicet . quod predicti Abbas ⁊ Conuentus de sancta cruce dabunt pro bono pacis singulis annis unum Bizancium uel duos
solidos ad festum sancti Martin ad luminaria inuenienda in ecclesia Sancti cuthberti De hales . ⁊ sic omnis controuersia inter ecclesias sancti
cuthberti De hales . ⁊ sancti cuthberti subtus Castellum de Edenesburc cessabit inperpetuum. Nos uero Iudices Delegati . hanc predictam ami
cabilem composicionem auctoritate Domini . pape . qua functi fuimus tempore istius Composicionis confirmauimus ⁊ sigillorum nostrorum corrobora
cione communiuimus . Vna Cum sigillis venerabilis patris nostri Episcopu Sancti Andree . ⁊ Capituli sui . ⁊ sigillis Abbatis ⁊ Conuentus de Dumf’.
⁊ sigillo Magistri . Ric’ rectoris dicte Ecclesie De Hales. Testibus . H . Episcopo Dunkeldensis . Magistro Laur’ Archidiacono Sancti Andree . Magistro
Matheo Cancellario Domin Regis . W . De Bosco . Archidiacono Laud’ . Rad’ Nigro decano Laud’ . Magistro petro de Rames’ . Gilleb’ de
Striuelin ⁊ Andrea clericis Domini Regis . Ric’ ⁊ hug’ clericis . W. De Bosco . Et Multis Aliis .

p.cl: John, by the grace of God abbot of Lindores, and Henry, by the same grace prior of St Andrews, and [blank space], prior of Lindores, to the whole body of Christ’s faithful people to whom the present written document might come, Eternal salvation in the Lord:
May all of you come to know that

since, by a mandate of the lord Pope, a certain case was set in motion between the abbot and convent of Dunfermline and Master Richard the rector of the church of Hailes on the one side, and the abbot and convent of Holyrood on the other, over the teinds of the Craigs of Gorgie, at length, with the consent of the parties, the dispute moved between the parties was amicably laid to rest, that is,

that the foresaid abbot and convent of Dunfermline, and Master Richard, the rector of the said church of Hailes, for themselves and their successors in perpetuity renounced the foresaid dispute in this form, that is, that the foresaid abbot and convent of Holyrood, for the sake of peace, shall each year give one bezant or two shillings at the feast of St Martin for the lights to be found in the church of St Cuthbert in Hailes, and so all controversies between the churches of St Cuthbert in Hailes and St Cuthbert beneath the Castle in Edinburgh shall cease in perpetuity.

We, the Judges Delegate, have confirmed and strengthened by the corroboration of our seals this foresaid amicable agreement by the authority of the lord Pope, by which we have been engaged at the time of this agreement, together with the seals of our venerable father the bishop of St Andrews, and of his chapter, and the seals of the abbot and convent of Dumfermline, and the seal of Master Richard the rector of the said church of Hailes.

As witnesses, Hugh the bishop of Dunkeld, Master Lawrence the archdeacon of St Andrews, Master Matthew the chancellor of the lord king, William del Bois the archdeacon of Lothian, Ralph Niger the dean of Lothian, Master Peter of Ramsay, Gilbert of Stirling and Andrew the clerks of the lord king, Richard and Hugh the clerks of William del Bois, and many others.
